title: hypericum lanceolatum subsp. angustifolium
intro: Hypericum lanceolatum subsp. angustifolium, also known as St. John's Wort, is a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and narrow, lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The health benefits of St. John's Wort include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve mood, and promote overall well-being, although its use has been linked to interactions with certain medications. Herbal preparations of St. John's Wort include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
